rationale: Within a ~4-second window, corp\Administrator executed five distinct domain user-enumeration commands from C:\Temp\ via a cmd.exe session: 'net user /domain' (A0), 'net users /domain' (EV-001), 'dsquery user' (EV-002), 'wmic /NAMESPACE:\\root\directory\ldap PATH ds_user GET ds_samaccountname' (EV-003), 'powershell get-wmiobject -class ds_user -namespace root\directory\ldap' (EV-004), and 'powershell Get-ADUser -Filter *' (EV-005). The rapid, sequential use of multiple redundant techniques to enumerate all domain users — spanning net.exe, dsquery, WMIC LDAP queries, and two PowerShell AD methods — is strongly indicative of automated or scripted Active Directory reconnaissance (T1087.002), not routine administrative activity. The working directory C:\Temp\ is atypical for legitimate admin tooling, and the breadth of methods used in under 4 seconds suggests a post-exploitation enumeration script rather than manual administration. No benign explanation accounts for this combination and velocity; however, no confirmed lateral movement or data exfiltration is evidenced in this package.
